one_line_thesis: PrairieSky is a high-margin royalty toll on Western Canadian drilling: no field operations, perpetual fee lands, and growing oil-weighted production from emerging plays (Clearwater, Mannville Stack, Duvernay).

deep_dive.executive_summary: PrairieSky is a high-margin royalty toll on Western Canadian drilling: no field operations, perpetual fee lands, and growing oil-weighted production from emerging plays (Clearwater, Mannville Stack, Duvernay). At C$34.50 (2026-07-20), we expect 6.06% per year on the total synthesis case (Lawrence owner-cash base 5.2%; bear -1.5%, bull 9.5%). FY2025 funds from operations of $1.50 per share and a $1.06 sustainable dividend anchor cash yield; Q1 2026 run-rate is higher at $0.41 per quarter. Moat is stable on land scarcity and operator relationships; dhando is partial because commodity prices a...

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Hurdle prices are derived from the decision-grade universal contract; they do not constitute a capital decision.

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: Scarce assets, royalties, and optionality
applicability_tests: The security owns a legally enforceable economic claim on scarce assets or royalties.
Material value is omitted or poorly represented by current GAAP earnings.
Asset quantities, ownership, overlap, timing, and remaining capital can be reconciled.
Comparable values can be adjusted for location, quality, control, liquidity, and realization time.
failure_modes: gross asset value mistaken for shareholder value
operating cash flow and NAV counted twice
remote options treated as contracted cash

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: The current component schedule is the first decision-grade baseline; attribution begins with the next dated valuation snapshot.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

economic_value.accounting_reference: Existing research and filing-derived valuation inputs. The primary-source reconciliation remains an open evidence gate.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of PSK.TO, including every currently identified operating claim, financial claim, liability, reserve, and material option.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Operating and asset claims are valued once; cash, debt, financing, reserves, and other senior claims are separate components or explicitly reserved. No component is intended to overlap another.

valuation_proof.evidence: FY2025 FFO $1.50/sh; Q1 2026 net debt $257.7M; shareholders equity $2,528M; E&E assets $1,468M; Q1 2026 lease bonus $12.3M; 232.4M shares (PSK.TO/official-reports/PSK-2025-YE-MDA.pdf; PSK.TO/official-reports/2026-Q1-MDA-FINAL.pdf; PSK.TO/official-reports/2026-Q1-Financial-Statements-FINAL.pdf). Proof: 21.37/sh base via owner_cash_or_dividend_discount@1.0.
valuation_proof.adjustment: The low/base/high range incorporates ownership, tax, capital, cycle, timing, and realization uncertainty pending the primary bridge.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key producing_royalty_stream; no other component capitalizes the same claim.

timeline.label: April 2026 AGM presentation: 18.6M royalty acres; oil royalty volume CAGR 6%/sh since Q1 2022
Q1 2026: FFO C$94.9M (C$0.41/sh); net debt C$257.7M; 201 third-party spuds on PrairieSky lands
Sustainable dividend raised to C$1.06/sh run rate (65% payout ratio Q1 2026)
AIF 2025 filed: PFIC/US withholding tax friction documented for cross-border holders
FY2025: FFO C$353.0M (C$1.50/sh); dividends C$1.04/sh; NCIB repurchases continued
Clearwater, Mannville Stack, and Duvernay play ramps drive oil-weighted royalty growth
FY2024 FFO C$380.5M peak before commodity normalization in FY2025
Spinoff from Encana as pure-play royalty vehicle (fee-simple mineral title model)

industry.structure: Western Canadian royalty and mineral-title owners compete for finite fee-simple acreage; PrairieSky is the largest passive royalty company with no operating wells or field capex.
industry.share_shift: Operators concentrate capital on Clearwater, Mannville Stack, and Duvernay plays on PrairieSky fee lands; third-party spuds drive volume without PrairieSky balance-sheet risk.
industry.trend: Oil-weighted royalty volumes growing while realized $/BOE reflects WCS differential and AECO gas volatility; lease bonus revenue remains lumpy.

top_claims.claim: PrairieSky is a high-margin royalty toll on Western Canadian drilling: no field operations, perpetual fee lands, and growing oil-weighted production from emerging plays (Clearwater, Mannville Stack, Duvernay). At C$34.46 (2026-06-03), we expect 6.06% per year on the total synthesis case (Lawrence owner-cash base 5.2%;
We expect **6.06%** per year over 7 years at **~$34.46** on **$1.5/sh** starting owner cash.
*Primary risk: Sustained weakness in Western Canadian realized oil and natural gas prices compresses funds from operations even if drilling activity remains strong.
Inversion: If WTI and AECO recover while spud counts hold, FFO per share could re-rate toward Q1 2026 run-rate ($1.64 annualized) without new acquisitions.

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: Sustained weakness in Western Canadian realized oil and natural gas prices compresses funds from operations even if drilling activity remains strong.
Inversion: If WTI and AECO recover while spud counts hold, FFO per share could re-rate toward Q1 2026 run-rate ($1.64 annualized) without new acquisitions.
Net debt ($257.7M) and bank facility draws limit flexibility in a prolonged downturn.
